ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-26-2025 Continued To Jan-27-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Victor Herrera for temporary successor trustee Scott C. Tolstad (via Zoom).
Drexel Bradshaw, objector and former trustee in pro per (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Third Account and Report of Administration, Petition for Settlement of Account, Approval of Compensation to Successor Trustee, Approval of Payments to Successor Trustee's Attorney, and for Final Distribution; and 2) Petition for Instructions on Distribution to Deceased Beneficiary.

The Court noted that at the previous hearing, Mr. Bradshaw had been instructed to file a petition if he intended to recover fees or other compensation from the trust. The Court observed that such a petition had not been filed.

Mr. Bradshaw stated that he had been seriously ill, and he experienced computer and connectivity issues that prevented him from filing the petition. He requested additional time and stated that he hoped to file by the close of business on Friday.

The Court asked Mr. Bradshaw to clarify his standing to object. Mr. Bradshaw responded that he was an interested party who had once held the conservatorship estate, and he claimed unpaid fees allegedly owed to his former counsel and to himself. He argued that the accounting was defective because the liabilities page listed no liabilities, and that his fee claims should have been listed as a liability. Mr. Bradshaw confirmed that this was the sole basis of his objection and that he sought an evidentiary hearing. He offered to produce attorney Sheila Robello as a witness to offer testimony regarding fee calculations, as well as his own testimony on expense reimbursements. The Court advised Mr. Bradshaw that he could not receive affirmative relief on an objection alone.

Regarding the petition for instructions, Mr. Herrera stated that a declaration was filed outlining efforts to locate beneficiaries. One beneficiary is confirmed deceased, and two others remain unverified. An ex parte request to release blocked funds is pending to retain an investigator. An investigator has not yet been retained for the missing beneficiaries. The Court indicated it would review the status of the request and that such use of funds would be appropriate to advance the matter.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to January 27, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Bradshaw was given a deadline of December 17, 2025 to file his petition seeking fees or any claim he intends to pursue. The Court advised that failure to file by that deadline would be construed as a waiver under the California Rules of Court.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Appearances: Victor Herrera, via Zoom, for Scott Tolstad, Successor Trustee.

The court notes its concern with a possible venue matter given that principal administration of the trust takes place in Escondido California. Counsel reports that the only asset of the trust is cash which is held in several banks in California. Counsel reports that an appeal in a related case is no longer pending and of the intent to file a third and final accounting if the instant petition is granted. The court reminds counsel that professional fiduciaries are typically allowed and hourly rate of up to $145 and the petition requests an hourly rate of $175. Counsel defers to the court to determine the professional fiduciaries hourly rate. The court and counsel discuss the request for the $777 payment which shows as having been paid but is not included in the accounting. Counsel advises that if the court is not inclined to grant the preceding, it can be stricken from the proposed order and that it will be included in the subsequent accounting. The court advises that it will take the preceding under consideration.

The matter is taken under submission.

Off Calendar Jul-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maryann Briseno, via ZOOM, counsel for Delores Coleman, Petitioner; Sheila Robello, via ZOOM, in propria persona Creditor; Victor Herrera, via ZOOM, counsel for Scott Tolstad, interim Successor Trustee.

At the outset, the court turns to Ms. Robello regarding whether she had served the parties with her reply to respondent's objection to her petition for fees. Ms. Robello reports that she had provided the parties with a copy of her reply via email. The court then queries Ms. Robello regarding whether there was an agreement between her and the parties regarding electronic service. Ms. Robello reports that due to the pandemic, she believed that electronic service was proper. All counsel confirm receipt of Ms. Robello's response to respondent's objection except for Ms. Brsieno. The court then confirms with Ms. Briseno if she wishes to proceed with the hearing. Ms. Briseno agrees to proceed, regardless. Ms. Robello then presents her arguments in support of the request for fees and states that some fees had been deferred to allow the trustee to remain in the home. Additionally, the services provided with regard to the reverse mortgage were required in order to obtain income for the trust and beneficiaries to remain in the home.

After additional discussion, the Amended Petition to Compel Payment of Attorney Fees, etc., is taken under submission.

Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sheila Robello appearing for Solan, Park and Robello;
Victor Herrera appearing for Scott Tolstad;
Drexel Bradshaw appearing in pro per.

Mr. Herrera stated that they need an order for the funds to be released to Mr. Tolstad. The Court stated that Schwab could put the funds in four separate CD accounts. Mr. Herrera stated that it was his understanding that the funds were to be placed in federally insured accounts. The Court directed Mr. Herrera to consult with Schwab regarding the separate CD accounts and then file a declaration with the court. Status on the declaration regarding the information from Schwab and the separate CD accounts is set for December 3, 2018 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Ms. Robello addressed her ex parte petition for withdrawal filed in July and argued in support of the payment of fees stating that she has court orders that backup the fees for her office and Mr. Bradshaw. Mr. Bradshaw addressed the State Bar hearing stating the there were no issues found, that Mr. Tolstad is to stand in contempt, that other fees haven't been challenged, and that the fees paid to Bay Construction for repairs on the Gosey property were fair. Mr. Bradsaw further stated that the State Bar found no misappropriation, that the work was done with proper permits, that the work was done at market rates or less than market rate, that the misrepresentation was misapprehension of the Probate court, that the ruling would likely be sometime in the Spring, and that the fees have not been objected to and should be paid. In addition, Mr. Bradshaw stated that Ms. Coleman does not get an insurance policy against his insolvency. Mr. Herrera stated that they are not opposed to making any payment and that they'd like to avoid any lawsuit. Mr. Bradshaw stated that Mr. Raxter did not join in this petition. Mr. Herrera noted that they had not seen anything from Mr. Raxter. Ms. Robello presented additional argument regarding the fees. The Court inquired whether the fees would be impacted by the trial in March. Ms. Robello responded "no". Ms. Robello provided additional argument in support of the fees stating that they have the court order, that they haven't filed anything, and that have they brought the petition to defer this payment/to protect any liability to them. Additional argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the petition for order instructing payment to Drexel Bradshaw and Solan, Park and Robello under submission. The status hearing on blocked accounts was continued to December 3, 2018 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeremiah Raxter for Delores Coleman (Petitioner).
David Baer for and with Drexel Bradshaw (Trustee).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether the petition is deficient based on the verification being signed on October 27th, whether or not admissible evidence of wrong doing is required for removal and/or suspension of a Trustee, whether or not use of the American Express card was inappropriate, etc. Counsel also spoke of Getty v. Getty, the State Bar hearing scheduled for January 16, 2018, the need for the property being sold for payment of the reverse mortgage, the issue of the sale proceeds being protected, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, to protect Trust assets the Court suspends Drexel Bradshaw as Trustee. The Court is not making any finding of wrong doing. Mr. Tolstad is appointed temporary Trustee with power to collect rent and sell Trust property with Court confirmation. Bond is set at $50,000.00. Sale proceeds are to be placed into a blocked account. Status on filing of a receipt for the blocked account is set for January 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Bradshaw is to turn over all assets within 10-days and turn over the keys by Wednesday. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. is continued to February 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, 877-771-3312.

Petition To: 1. Subject Trust To Probate Court Supervision; 2. Remove Drexel Bradshaw As Trustee And Direct Delivery Of Assets ... Et Al.
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Continued To Dec-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeremiah Raxter for Delores Coleman (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
Sheila Robello and David Baer for and with Drexel Bradshaw (Trustee).

In light of the animosity between the Trustee and the Beneficiary, the Court questions why Mr. Bradshaw does not want to resign. Mr. Baer stated that Mr. Bradshaw has done nothing wrong, that he made a commitment to Ms. Gosey, that him resigning will not make litigation go away and that he does not believe there is any personal enmity. Mr. Baer also stated that a private fiduciary would be costly to the Trust, that it is not a complex matter or any day to day interaction, that there is no evidence that Mr. Bradshaw has done anything wrong and that it is inconsistent with the Trustor's wishes. Mr. Raxter stated that Mr. Bradshaw is taking fees over $100,000.00, that Mr. Bradshaw has two Attorneys, that there is a reverse mortgage on the property so it has to be sold, and that a Fiduciary should be appointed to sell the house and place the funds into a blocked account. Mr. Raxter also stated that removal of Mr. Bradshaw is to protect the Trust and the Beneficiaries, and that Mr. Bradshaw is draining the Trust. Mr. Baer stated that Mr. Bradshaw received a Court order to take conservatorship fees of $19,000.00 three years ago, that he did not take it, that in 2015 he received an order for Trustees fees of $14,000.00 which he has not taken either, that there would need to be some discovery and that they will probably be ready to go to trial sometime in March. Mr. Raxter stated that the trial would be three to four days long, that he has some discovery to propound by the end of next week, and that they have to sell the property by March or April. The Petition to Subject Trust to Probate Court Supervision, etc. is continued to December 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and setting of trial in Dept. 206. Mr. Baer was informed that his request to dissolve the September ex parte order would need to be presented in the form of a petition for Judge Stewart to hear.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, 877-771-3312.
